title: William Spence — Galveston Immigration Database Search
subject: William Spence (oral tradition: Hunter's great-grandfather)

oral_tradition.name: William Spence
oral_tradition.arrival_detail: came from England when he was 8 years old
oral_tradition.note: Maybe the boat just came there — possibly born Scotland, English port only

results.name: CHARLES A. SPENCE
results.source: NATIONAL ARCHIVES RECORD GROUP 85, MICROCOPY 1359, ROLL #20
results.assessment: NOT HUNTER'S ANCESTOR — age 24, seaman, not a child immigrant

results.name: WILLIAM SPENCE
results.source: NATIONAL ARCHIVES RECORD GROUP 85, MICROCOPY 1359, ROLL #20
results.assessment: POSSIBLE — name matches, arrived near Beaumont/Port Arthur area; age 19 conflicts with oral tradition of 'age 8'; could be same person with misremembered age, or could have jumped ship and settled in the area
results.notes: Three Spences all on same ship from Cuba — likely British merchant marine crew; could have settled in Port Arthur oil industry after arrival

results.name: ALFRED SPENCE
results.source: NATIONAL ARCHIVES RECORD GROUP 85, MICROCOPY 1359, ROLL #20
results.assessment: NOT HUNTER'S ANCESTOR — name Alfred, not William

databases_searched.source: Galveston County District Clerk Immigration Portal

key_finding.summary: Only one William Spence ever arrived at Galveston from Great Britain — record #71298, age 19, seaman, April 24 1920, ship KUNA, arrived at Port Arthur TX (adjacent to Beaumont). Name and geography match. Age does not match oral tradition.
key_finding.age_conflict: oral tradition says age 8, record shows age 19
key_finding.plausibility: A 19-year-old British seaman arriving at Port Arthur in 1920 could have jumped ship, stayed in the Spindletop oil boom area, married, and fathered Dale Sr. in 1934 (age ~33). Plausible but unconfirmed.
key_finding.oral_tradition_explanation: Possible that 'came from England at age 8' is either: (a) misremembered — conflated with a different family story, (b) refers to his father's immigration not his own, or (c) a completely different William Spence came through New York not Galveston and no Galveston record exists

next_steps.action: Order Texas DSHS birth certificate for Dale Spence Sr.
next_steps.why: Would definitively name Dale Sr.'s parents, confirming or denying William Spence as father
next_steps.info_needed: Dale Sr. full name, exact birthdate (~1934-1936), birthplace (Beaumont TX)

next_steps.action: Search National Archives microfilm M1359 Roll #20 for full KUNA passenger manifest
next_steps.why: Full manifest would show William Spence's hometown in UK, allowing FreeBMD search
next_steps.how: NARA in Fort Worth TX (regional) or ancestry.com immigration records

next_steps.action: Search 1930 US Census Jefferson County TX for Spence family, British-born head
next_steps.why: If William settled in area after 1920, would appear in 1930 census (4 years before Dale Sr. born)
next_steps.how: Ancestry.com (paid) or Beaumont public library free census access

next_steps.action: Contact Tyrrell Historical Library, Beaumont TX
next_steps.why: Local historical newspaper archives may contain Spence family references; vital records microfilm

next_steps.action: Search FreeBMD for Spence male births 1900-1910 in County Durham, Yorkshire, or Scottish border counties
next_steps.why: If William Spence born ~1901 in NE England, birth would be in FreeBMD

confidence.william_spence_as_ancestor: POSSIBLE (oral tradition name match, geographic match) — NOT CONFIRMED
confidence.galveston_1920_record_as_same_person: LOW-MEDIUM (age discrepancy is significant; could be different person or misremembered oral tradition)
confidence.overall_note: Brick wall. Documentary evidence required — DSHS birth certificate is the fastest path to breaking it.
